availability.summary: A bounded award-download probe returned a fresh file_name, and a follow-up GET to /api/v2/download/status returned HTTP 200 with a live running status payload on 2026-05-12.

sources.title: Current raw MCP profile for download status
Download status view and shared download URL builder
Download job lookup table and elapsed-time model method
Live download creation and status probes run on 2026-05-12

parameters.description: Exact download job ZIP filename returned by a prior async download endpoint response.

documented.notes: Taken from the file_name field of a download endpoint response.

observed.notes: A fresh file_name from the award download endpoint was accepted on 2026-05-12. Current raw profile also recorded 400 errors for blank or missing values and 404 errors for unknown filenames.

constraints: Must match a known download job filename exactly.
Blank or omitted values are rejected as missing.
Unknown or expired filenames return a 404 detail error instead of a status payload.

templates.name: poll_download_job_status
templates.description: Poll the status of a previously started download job using the exact file_name returned by that job-creation endpoint.

response.shapeSummary: A status object for one asynchronous download job on HTTP 200, or a small error object containing only detail for bad or unknown file_name values.

fields.description: Current backend job state name for the requested download job.

observed.notes: A fresh 2026-05-12 probe returned running; the current raw profile previously observed running and finished. Source code and lookup tables show the API returns raw job_status.name values, which include additional names such as queued, resumed, created, and uploading.

constraints: Treat the value as an open-ended backend status string rather than a closed four-value enum.
Do not assume undocumented nonterminal statuses are impossible.

fields.description: Nullable human-readable error message associated with a failed download job; otherwise typically null.

documented.notes: Documentation says it is null unless status is failed.

observed.notes: A live running response returned null; failed-state content remains unobserved in this bundle.

constraints: Presence of a non-null message is most relevant when the job status indicates failure.

fields.description: ZIP filename identifying the requested download job.
Download URL for the generated archive.

documented.notes: The staged example shows a relative /usaspending-api/csv_downloads path.

observed.notes: Fresh and prior observed responses used absolute HTTPS URLs, which matches the non-local source implementation.

constraints: Use the URL exactly as returned; do not prepend the API host.

fields.description: Archive size in kilobytes when known.

documented.notes: Docs say the field is null if the job is not finished.

observed.notes: The fresh running probe returned null, and the current raw profile previously recorded a numeric value once the job finished.

constraints: Null indicates the backend has not yet computed or stored a file size.

fields.description: Column count across the generated CSV content when the backend has populated it.

documented.notes: Docs say this is null until finished.

observed.notes: The fresh running probe returned 0 rather than null, the current raw profile previously recorded numeric counts while running and finished, and source code does not gate this field on finished status.

constraints: Treat counts before a terminal status as provisional.

fields.description: Row count across the generated CSV content when the backend has populated it.

documented.notes: Docs say this is null until finished.

observed.notes: The fresh running probe returned 0 rather than null, the current raw profile previously recorded a nonzero running count, and source code returns the stored field directly.

constraints: Treat counts before a terminal status as provisional.

fields.description: Elapsed processing time represented as a stringified duration or numeric-seconds-like string in observed responses.

observed.notes: Source code returns a Python timedelta and observed API responses serialize it as a string.

constraints: Parse defensively as a string rather than assuming a JSON number.

fields.description: Error detail returned instead of the status payload for missing, blank, or unknown file_name requests.

examples: Missing one or more required query parameters: file_name
Download job with filename foo does not exist.
observed.notes: This field is absent from the staged docs but present in current raw profile error examples and implied by source exceptions.

constraints: Error responses may contain only detail and omit all success fields.

notes: The raw MCP profile already exposes the only material request parameter.
The semantic bundle adds contradiction handling for status enum completeness, absolute file_url behavior, error-only detail responses, and the shared bulk_download/status alias.

contradictions.statement: The documentation treats status as a four-value enum, but the backend returns the raw job_status.name and the lookup table defines additional states such as queued, resumed, created, and uploading.
contradictions.impact: Clients should treat status as an open-ended backend state string and handle undocumented nonterminal values gracefully.

contradictions.statement: The staged docs show file_url as a relative path, while current raw profile observations, fresh live responses, and non-local source code indicate an absolute storage URL.
contradictions.impact: Use file_url as returned and do not prepend the API host.

contradictions.statement: The staged docs say total_rows and total_columns are null until finished, but fresh and prior running responses contained numeric counts and source code does not gate those fields on terminal status.
contradictions.impact: Counts in nonterminal states should be treated as provisional rather than absent.

quirks.statement: The endpoint returns download-job metadata rather than the exported CSV rows themselves.
quirks.impact: Use it to poll readiness and discover file_url, not to inspect spending records.

quirks.statement: Bad requests and unknown filenames can return a compact error object with only detail instead of the documented status payload.
quirks.impact: Response parsing must branch on HTTP status or the presence of detail.

quirks.statement: The same backend view is reachable through both /api/v2/download/status and /api/v2/bulk_download/status.
quirks.impact: file_name values from bulk and row-limited download workflows can be polled through the same logic.

gaps.statement: This bundle did not live-observe a failed job response, so non-null message content and exact failure timing remain unresolved.
gaps.impact: Consumers should treat failed-job message text as opaque until observed in a future run.

gaps.statement: Undocumented statuses beyond ready, running, finished, and failed are source-visible but were not live-observed in this run.
gaps.impact: The endpoint surface should remain open to additional status values even if current callers usually see running or finished.

risks.statement: Polling with stale or mistyped file_name values frequently yields 404 detail errors because the endpoint does not search for near matches.
risks.impact: Preserve the exact file_name returned by the initiating download endpoint.

risks.statement: Row and column counts can change before the job reaches a terminal state.
risks.impact: Do not report nonterminal totals as final analytic facts.
